query_results.answer:
There is no approved national early detection screening program for skin cancer in the UK, but private clinics in London offer thorough head-to-toe skin examinations by specialist nurses or dermatologists.

### Recommended Private Skin Cancer Screening Practices in London

| Practice | Location | One-Line Reason |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **The Mole Clinic** | Multiple UK sites (including London) | Advanced Skin Cancer Screening Nurses perform thorough head-to-toe checks; no GP referral needed [2]. |
| **The London General Practice** | Central London | Offers full skin checks with video review by a dermatologist for concerning lesions; part of comprehensive health screens [1]. |
| **Skin Care Network** | London | Believes annual screening is essential; provides bespoke screening for high-risk patients via visual inspection by experienced specialists [3]. |
| **Dr. Magnus Lynch (Private)** | London Bridge Hospital, Skin55 (Harley St), OneWelbeck | Head-to-toe examination creating a baseline image for monitoring; provides detailed written records and plans [5]. |
| **The Online GP by The Wellness** | Marylebone (near Harley St) | Premier doctor-led pathway with same-day appointments; includes dermatoscopic mole checks and full-body mole mapping [6][8]. |
| **The London Clinic (Harley Street)** | 142-146 Harley Street | Experienced dermatologists assess moles and lesions for prompt identification; located in the heart of Harley Street [7]. |
| **London Skin Clinic** | Central London | Uses advanced dermoscopy and digital mole mapping; claims 99% five-year survival when melanoma is caught at stage one [9]. |
| **Guy's and St Thomas' NHS** | Guy's Hospital (Great Maze Pond) | Public NHS option with a dedicated "Mole screening clinic" and "Skin cancer screening clinic" (requires referral) [10]. |

### Key Information for Your Appointment
*   **No National Program:** The UK lacks a national screening program because there is no instant medical test; visual inspection by a specialist is the only accurate method [1][3].
*   **Preparation:** Remove makeup and nail polish (skin cancers can occur on nail beds) and be ready to discuss your personal/family history of skin cancer [5].
*   **Referral:** Most private clinics (e.g., The Mole Clinic, The Online GP) do **not** require a GP referral; you can book directly [2][6].
*   **Process:** You will likely be asked to undress to underwear (a gown is provided) for a full head-to-toe check, including areas difficult to see yourself [5].

For urgent concerns, same-day appointments are often available at private clinics like **The Online GP** [6]. If you prefer NHS care, contact **Guy's and St Thomas'** for their screening clinics, though a referral is typically required [10].

For the most advanced care, **OneWelbeck Skin Health & Allergy** (Marylebone) is London’s most advanced centre for dermatology and allergy care, offering state-of-the-art treatment[7].

For a mole check in London, the **Cadogan Clinic** in Chelsea is a top recommendation as it pioneered mole mapping in the UK and holds the first mole check approved by the British Skin Foundation[1].

Here are the best verified options for a mole check clinic in London:

| Clinic | Location | Key Reason |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Cadogan Clinic** | 120 Sloane Street, Chelsea | UK pioneer of mole mapping; first mole check approved by British Skin Foundation[1] |
| **The Mole Clinic** | 5 locations (London Bridge, Oxford Circus, Harley Street, Moorgate, Richmond) | Leading independent skin cancer screening clinic; single check £60, full body £195[3] |
| **London Doctors Clinic** | Oxford Street & Liverpool Street | Specialist-led checks by experienced dermatologists (Dr Ghazavi, Dr Hasan, Dr Rafiq)[2] |
| **Chiswick Clinic** | West London | Led by Dr. Bela (26+ years experience); focuses on early melanoma detection[5] |
| **Cosmedics Skin Clinics** | Harley Street, City, Putney | Full body screening £200; offers specific mole consultation for removal prep[6] |
| **Dermasurge** | Harley Street | Expert dermatologist Dr. Hiba Injibar uses dermatoscopy for detailed mole assessment[7] |
| **London Dermatology Centre** | 69 Wimpole Street, Central London | Specialist clinic for abnormal mole detection; 6-min walk from Bond Street[8] |
| **Mole Check Removal** | Harley Street & City (Queen Street) | Same-day urgent screening; consultant dermatoscopy with written report, no GP referral needed[10] |

**Quick Tips:**
*   **Costs:** Single mole checks range from **£60** (The Mole Clinic)[3] to **£250** (Cadogan Clinic)[1], while full-body screenings are typically **£195–£200**[3][6].
*   **Referrals:** Most clinics (e.g., Mole Check Removal, Cadogan) do **not require a GP referral** for booking[1][10].
*   **Urgency:** For urgent same-day checks, **Mole Check Removal** (Harley Street/City) offers immediate appointments with written reports[10].

Choose **Cadogan Clinic** for comprehensive, award-winning screening, or **The Mole Clinic** for affordable, accessible options across multiple London locations.

query_results.answer:
You can book an appointment with a **private dermatologist in London** without needing a GP referral at several top clinics, including **Cleveland Clinic London**, **Cromwell Hospital**, and **St John & St Elizabeth Hospital**.

### Recommended Private Dermatology Practices in London

| Practice | Location | Key Reason |
|----------|----------|------------|
| **Cleveland Clinic London** | Heart of London (Marylebone) | Modern, technologically advanced facilities; self-pay and insured options; online booking available [1] |
| **Cromwell Hospital** | Cromwell Road, SW5 | World-renowned consultant dermatologists including Dr James Shelley and Dr Raj Mallipeddi; skin cancer expertise [6] |
| **St John & St Elizabeth Hospital** | St John’s Wood, NW8 | Same-day/next-day appointments for acne, warts, skin cancer; self-referral allowed [5] |
| **Guy's and St Thomas' Specialist Care** | Harley Street Health District | 150+ years of skin condition treatment at St John’s Institute; private outpatient facility [3] |
| **The London Clinic** | Harley Street, W1G | Only private hospital with 24/7 same-day GP access; advanced imaging for dermatology [4] |
| **Dr.Dropin** | Fulham Road, SW6 | Flexible evening/weekend slots; video consultations; no referral needed; same-day booking [9] |
| **Kensington International Clinic** | Knightsbridge, SW7 | Multilingual consultations (English, French, Arabic, etc.); near Harrods and V&A Museum [8] |
| **Canary Wharf Clinic** | Canary Wharf, E14 | Expert private dermatology in business district; open Mon–Sat 9am–7pm [10] |

### How to Book
- **Self-pay**: Most clinics accept direct payment (e.g., Cleveland Clinic: +44 20 3423 7500 [1]; Cromwell: +44 20 7244 4886 [6]).
- **Insured**: Contact your provider first; clinics like Cromwell and Cleveland offer insured pathways [1][6].
- **Online**: Cleveland Clinic, Dr.Dropin, and Canary Wharf Clinic allow online appointment booking [1][9][10].
- **No Referral Needed**: You can self-refer to any private dermatologist in the UK [5][9].

For urgent concerns (e.g., skin cancer, cysts), **St John & St Elizabeth** and **Dr.Dropin** offer same-day appointments [5][9]. For comprehensive care with top specialists, **Cromwell Hospital** and **Cleveland Clinic London** are highly recommended [1][6].
